query_string: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame

bonuses.leadin: Answer some questions about American performance venues for non-classical musicians, for 10 points each:
parts: I.M. Pei designed this pyramid-shaped building that houses exhibits and memorabilia from artists such as The Beatles and Elvis Presley. This Cleveland venue features a first-floor stage and a third-floor theatre for performances.

This radio show was once broadcast from Ryman Auditorium, and performances are now taped in a namesake “House” in Nashville. The weekly stage concerts with this name are critical for fledgling folk and country musicians.

Boxer Jack Johnson opened this Harlem jazz establishment, which began as a “Whites Only” club despite employing primarily black musicians. Duke Ellington’s residency here is the subject of the musical After Midnight.
answers: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Museum
The Grand Ole Opry [prompt on “Opry”; do NOT accept answers containing “Old”]
answers_sanitized: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Museum
The Grand Ole Opry [prompt on "Opry"; do NOT accept answers containing "Old"]

This radio show was once broadcast from Ryman Auditorium, and performances are now taped in a namesake "House" in Nashville. The weekly stage concerts with this name are critical for fledgling folk and country musicians.

Boxer Jack Johnson opened this Harlem jazz establishment, which began as a "Whites Only" club despite employing primarily black musicians. Duke Ellington's residency here is the subject of the musical After Midnight.
